message:
fix(tools/identity): tool-layer retry for update_agent_registration_token (#105)

Issue #105 documented a caller-visible write failure during
\`register_agent\`: the single UPDATE that stamps a new
registration token raced with a transient degraded-storage window
and immediately surfaced \`RESOURCE_BUSY\` to the MCP client. The
client then had to retry the entire \`register_agent\` call,
losing the freshly-generated token on the first attempt — a
confusing failure mode for anyone integrating the tool server.

The DB layer already runs its own MVCC retry budget on this
statement, but the tool-layer call site did not mirror the coarse
4-attempt / 500, 1000, 1500, 2000 ms retry ladder that the
surrounding \`register_agent\` tool uses. Mirror it for this
specific write so a transient busy window settles inside the
server process rather than bubbling out to the client.

Changes in \`crates/mcp-agent-mail-tools/src/identity.rs\`:

- New private async helper
  \`persist_agent_registration_token_with_retry(ctx, pool,
  agent_id, agent_name, registration_token)\`.
  - Runs a \`loop\` with \`UPDATE_TOKEN_TOOL_RETRIES = 4\` as the
    coarse retry budget, mirroring the \`register_agent\` ladder
    exactly.
  - Delegates each attempt to the existing
    \`mcp_agent_mail_db::queries::update_agent_registration_token\`,
    so the DB-layer MVCC retry still runs unchanged inside each
    tool-layer attempt.
  - Gates retry on the shared \`is_register_retryable(err)\`
    classifier, so the wrapper will NEVER retry on a hard error
    (schema mismatch, permissions, etc.). Only lock/busy errors
    get the ladder.
  - Reuses \`register_agent_retry_sleep_ms(attempt, now_ns)\`
    with a \`now_ns\` pulled from \`SystemTime\` so jittered
    sleeps share the same randomization source the rest of the
    register lane uses.
  - Sleeps via \`asupersync::time::sleep(ctx.cx().now(), ...)\`
    so cooperative shutdown and cancellation behave the same as
    elsewhere in the tool.
  - Emits a \`warn!\` with \`attempt\`, \`max\`, and the agent
    name on every retry so operators can see the ladder firing in
    logs if it becomes a latency issue.

- Both \`register_agent\` call sites that previously invoked
  \`mcp_agent_mail_db::queries::update_agent_registration_token\`
  directly now call the new helper with the already-resolved
  \`agent_id\` and \`row.name\`. No new allocations; \`row.name\`
  is borrowed for the warn! payload and the token string is
  already in scope.

Pure transient-retry hardening. No schema change, no API change,
no change to non-busy error paths — a genuine conflict or invalid
token still fails on the first attempt.
